name: list
description: Plain bullet list under a heading.
purpose: Use only when the items are genuinely a list (5–6 short points). For anything richer, prefer cards-grid, cards-stack, or list-tabular.

items.description: List items. Keep each under ~12 words.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: list -->

## Slide heading.

- First short bullet point.
- Second short bullet point.
- Third short bullet point.
- Fourth short bullet point.
- Fifth short bullet point.
sample:
<!-- _class: list -->

## What the first six months of pilots taught us.

- Teams log roughly one decision for every twenty they actually make.
- The scoring weights get re-tuned after almost every retrospective.
- Predicted outcomes are the field most often left blank.
- Alignment scores rose fastest on the teams that reviewed the log weekly.
- No pilot team has asked to go back to the old process.

whenToUse.title: Genuinely a list
whenToUse.body: Five to six short points, each under twelve words. No internal structure per item — just a heading and the bullets.

whenToUse.title: Numbered when order matters
whenToUse.body: Use `ol` (`1.` source) when sequence is load-bearing; `ul` when order is interchangeable. Numbers render as a tabular leading column.

whenToUse.title: Pills via inline code
whenToUse.body: Inline code at the end of a row becomes a pill (status tag, metric, owner). Lets the list double as a lightweight ledger without changing layout.

antiPatterns.title: Title plus body per item
antiPatterns.body: If each bullet is `**Title.** body`, the layout under-serves it. Move to cards-stack (2-3 items) or list-tabular (5+ rows) instead.

antiPatterns.title: Wall of long bullets
antiPatterns.body: Past twelve words per line the slide becomes paragraph soup. Either trim or move to content for prose, cards-stack for structured items.

antiPatterns.title: Two-item lists
antiPatterns.body: Two bullets read as a thin slide. For pairs, reach for cards-side or compare-prose — both give the pair the weight it deserves.

related.name: tldr
related.when: single-line takeaways at the end of a section

related.name: cards-stack
related.when: each item has a title plus body sentence

related.name: list-tabular
related.when: five or more rows with label-plus-description

related.name: checklist
related.when: items carry state markers (done / partial / todo)
